name: Apricot Dressing for Turkey
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Chopped walnuts or pine nuts
12 slices Bread, dried and cut into small cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring the dried apricots and water (or chicken/turkey stock) to a boil.
2. Allow the mixture to stand for 10 minutes.
3. Add the chopped celery, walnuts or pine nuts, and bread cubes to the saucepan. Toss lightly to moisten the bread and blend the ingredients.
4. Transfer the mixture into an oiled baking dish with a cover.
5. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for about 40 minutes.
6. Remove the cover during the last 10 minutes of baking to allow the top of the dressing to brown.
1/12 recipe - 118 calories, 1 bread serving, half fat, half fruit exchange, 22 grams carbohydrates, 4 grams protein, 3 grams fat, 127 mg sodium, 215 mg potassium, 0 cholesterol
Source: Am. Diabetes Assoc. Holiday Cookbook by Betty Wedman, 1986. Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Nov 93
